faqs.question: What are the most common pantry pests in Corona homes?
faqs.answer: Corona residents most frequently encounter Indian meal moths, grain beetles, weevils, and flour beetles. These pests infest dry goods like cereal, flour, rice, pasta, nuts, and dried fruits. They often enter homes through contaminated groceries or can migrate between apartments in multi-unit buildings common throughout Corona.

faqs.question: How quickly can you provide pantry pest control service in Corona?
faqs.answer: We offer same-day pantry pest control service throughout Corona, Queens. Our technicians can typically arrive within hours of your call to assess the infestation, identify the source, and begin treatment. We understand that pantry pests contaminate food quickly, so we prioritize rapid response for Corona residents.

faqs.question: What does your $350-$550 pantry pest control service include?
faqs.answer: Our service includes a thorough inspection of your kitchen and pantry areas, identification of infested products, targeted treatment using safe pesticides, removal recommendations, preventive measures, and sealing of entry points. Price varies based on infestation severity and property size. We also provide guidance on proper food storage to prevent re-infestation.

faqs.question: How can I prevent pantry pests from returning after treatment?
faqs.answer: Store all dry goods in airtight containers, inspect groceries before storing them, clean pantry shelves regularly, rotate older items to the front, and dispose of any expired products. We'll provide detailed prevention tips specific to your Corona home during our service visit and can schedule follow-up inspections to ensure your pantry remains pest-free.
